statement.p: To estimate the health of the black-tailed deer population in the Jewell Meadow Wildlife Area, the Oregon Department of Fish and Wildlife caught, tagged, and released 85 black-tailed deer. A month later, they returned and observed 67 black-tailed deer, 6 of which had tags. Approximately how many black-tailed deer are in the Jewell Meadow Wildlife Area?

solution.p: Let x represent how many deer are in the Jewell Meadow Area. We set up a proportional equation:

p.me: \begin{aligned} \frac{\text{tagged deer}}{\text{total deer}} \amp= \frac{\text{tagged deer}}{\text{total deer}}\\ \frac{6}{67} \amp= \frac{85}{x}\\ \frac{6}{67} \multiplyright{67\cdot x} \amp= \frac{85}{x}\multiplyright{67\cdot x}\\ 6 \cdot x \amp= 85\cdot 67\\ \divideunder{6 \cdot x}{6} \amp= \divideunder{85\cdot 67}{6}\\ x \amp= 949 \end{aligned}

solution.p: There are approximately 949 deer in the Jewell Meadow Area.
